stem: In June, the price of a vintage poster was $24.00. In July, the price of the vintage poster was $26.40. The price of the vintage poster in July was p% of the price of the vintage poster in June. What is the value of p?
rationale: Choice D is correct. The July price as a percent of the June price equals (26.40/24.00) × 100% = 1.1 × 100% = 110%. So p = 110. Choice A is incorrect; 10 would be the percent increase, not the price ratio expressed as a percent. Choice B is incorrect; 90 would correspond to the July price being 90% of the June price (a decrease). Choice C is incorrect; 190 corresponds to nearly doubling the price.
